title: SpEL expressions should have a valid syntax
description: Ensures that Spring Expression Language (SpEL) expressions used in annotations or configuration are syntactically correct, preventing runtime errors and unexpected behavior.
recommendation: Validate all SpEL expressions at development time to catch syntax errors before deployment. Use IDE support or static analysis tools to verify correctness.
impact: Invalid SpEL expressions can cause application failures at runtime, leading to bugs that are hard to diagnose and potentially impacting application stability.

examples.bad: @Value("#{user.name}") // if 'user' or 'name' is not defined, or syntax is wrong

checks.message: Validate SpEL expressions in @Value annotations for proper syntax. Consider using safe navigation operators or checking for null values.
